title: potamogeton × fluitans proles novaeboracensis
intro: Potamogeton × fluitans proles novaeboracensis, commonly known as New York floating-leaf pondweed, is a hybrid aquatic plant that is a cross between Potamogeton fluitans and P. perfoliatus. It is a submerged aquatic plant found in freshwater environments, primarily in the Great Lakes region. The New York floating-leaf pondweed has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. The plant is used in herbal preparations, often in the form of tinctures or infusions, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health.
